# Blueprint v0.3.3 — uninstaller actually wipes data + "Run Blueprint" on install

Two fixes for the install/uninstall flow from continued testing.

## "Delete user data" on uninstall now actually deletes everything

v0.3.2 added a prompt — "Also delete your Blueprint user data?" —
but Yes-clicks left state behind. Reinstalls still skipped the
welcome wizard and showed previously-pulled models as already on
disk.

Root cause: the NSIS uninstaller wiped `$PROFILE\.blueprint` BEFORE
stopping the Windows Service. The supervisor still had open file
handles on `svc-token`, logs, and the GGUF it was actively serving;
`RMDir /r` is best-effort on Windows and silently skips locked
files. Result: a partial wipe that looked like the prompt didn't
work.

Fixed by reordering the uninstall steps:

1. Stop + unregister `blueprint-svc` (Windows Service) first
2. Sleep ~1.5s to let the SCM release handles
3. THEN ask about user data
4. On Yes, wipe both `$PROFILE\.blueprint` AND
   `$PROGRAMDATA\Blueprint` (the second one is where the service
   writes its config + status + logs; leaving it caused a
   reinstall to immediately try spawning llama-server against a
   model path the wipe just deleted)

If you previously clicked Yes in v0.3.2 and ended up with leftover
state, the v0.3.3 uninstaller will clean it up cleanly on the next
attempt.

## Install-complete page now offers "Run Blueprint now"

Added the standard NSIS `MUI_FINISHPAGE_RUN` checkbox to the
install-finished page. Checked by default — finish the installer
and the app opens immediately. No more hunting for the Start Menu
shortcut.

## How to test the fix

1. Install v0.3.3 (the installer page after install-complete shows
   the new checkbox)
2. Use Blueprint, pull a model, etc.
3. From Windows Settings → Apps → Blueprint → Uninstall
4. On the data-deletion prompt, click Yes
5. Reinstall v0.3.3
6. You should now see the welcome wizard AND an empty Models list —
   confirming the wipe was complete this time

## Other platforms

macOS / Linux are unchanged — they don't have an installer-driven
uninstall flow, so neither this fix nor the "Run on completion"
applies there. The Maintain tab's **Reset Blueprint data** button
remains the cross-platform path for wiping data without
uninstalling.
